headline: 'We are walking a fine line': Incurably ill patients on France's new assisted-dying law
subhead: A law creating a right to aid in dying was adopted by French lawamkers on Wednesday. Le Monde spoke with people facing serious illness about how the choice weighs on their lives.

subhead: The administration is celebrating the success of one of its rare redistributive measures, which was launched on July 6. Just months ahead of the midterm elections, this highly popular initiative is causing discomfort among Democrats.

headline: Christopher Nolan struggles to match Homer in 'The Odyssey'
subhead: In his search for an original aesthetic capable of capturing the epic masterpiece of antiquity, the filmmaker opts for grandeur, with a prestigious cast and vast resources, but fails to convince.

headline: Argentina beat England 2-1 to reach World Cup 2026 final
subhead: Argentina will have a chance to become the first team since Brazil in 1962 to win back-to-back World Cups after Lionel Messi, 39, once again dragged his team to victory.

headline: As her deputy questions promise to ban headscarves, Le Pen doubles down
subhead: Jordan Bardella has expressed doubts about one of the Rassemblement National's key proposals. But Marine Le Pen is considering putting it to a referendum, if she is elected.

headline: Judge of 2016 Nice terror attack trial reflects on 'abominable' case, 10 years after
subhead: Laurent Raviot presided over the Paris special criminal court that held the trial of the attack in Nice that killed 86 people on July 14, 2016. On the 10th anniversary of the attack, he granted Le Monde his first interview, speaking with unusual candor.

headline: Visualize the full extent of wildfires across Europe in recent weeks
subhead: The burned areas across the European continent have already far surpassed levels seen in recent years and are now approaching the maximum recorded in 2025.
